description: Multi-Buffer Management & Window Splitting - Emacs-like multi-buffer, minibuffer input, and vertical window splitting

userStories.title: Internal multi-buffer management and command-line support
userStories.description: As a developer, I need goomacs to hold multiple buffers internally so that users can have several files open at once.
acceptanceCriteria: main.go holds a buffer list ([]*Buffer) and an active buffer index instead of a single *Buffer
Multiple files can be specified via command-line arguments (goomacs file1.go file2.go); first file is active
When no arguments are given, starts with a single empty buffer named *scratch* (preserves existing behavior)
Each buffer maintains independent cursor position, scroll offset, modified flag, and undo history
All existing keybindings (editing, movement, search, save, undo, kill/yank) work on the active buffer
C-x C-s saves the active buffer
C-x C-c checks all buffers for unsaved changes; if any are modified, prompts 'Modified buffers exist; exit anyway? (y/n)'
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundational change. Replace the single 'buf' variable with a buffers slice and activeBufferIdx. Update all references to use the active buffer. The redraw closure, search mode, C-x prefix handling, and status line must all work with the active buffer from the list.

userStories.title: Generic minibuffer input mode
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable minibuffer input mode so that buffer switch, file open, and buffer close can all prompt the user for text input.
acceptanceCriteria: A minibuffer input mode with state variables: mode flag, prompt string, input buffer ([]rune), and a callback function to invoke on Enter
When active, the message line shows the prompt followed by user input
Typing rune characters appends to the input buffer
Backspace removes the last character from the input buffer
Enter invokes the callback with the current input string and exits minibuffer mode
C-g cancels minibuffer mode without invoking the callback
All other keys are ignored while in minibuffer mode
Minibuffer mode takes priority over normal key dispatch (similar to searchMode)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Model this after the existing searchMode pattern in main.go. Add minibufferMode bool, minibufferPrompt string, minibufferInput []rune, minibufferCallback func(string). The callback pattern allows different commands (C-x b, C-x C-f, C-x k) to reuse the same input mode.

userStories.title: Switch buffer via minibuffer (C-x b)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch buffers by typing a buffer name so that I can quickly jump to a known file.
acceptanceCriteria: C-x b activates minibuffer mode with prompt 'Switch to buffer: '
Typing a buffer name and pressing Enter switches the active buffer to the named buffer
If the entered name does not match any existing buffer, a new empty buffer with that name is created and made active
If input is empty on Enter, switches to the previous buffer (the buffer that was active before the current one)
C-g cancels the operation
Status line updates to show the new buffer's filename after switching
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Track a 'previousBufferIdx' or 'alternateBuffer' to enable quick switching with empty input. Use the minibuffer callback from US-002.

userStories.title: Open file from editor (C-x C-f)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open a new file from within the editor so that I don't have to restart goomacs.
acceptanceCriteria: C-x C-f activates minibuffer mode with prompt 'Find file: '
Typing a file path and pressing Enter loads the file into a new buffer and makes it active
If the file is already open in an existing buffer, switches to that buffer instead of creating a duplicate
If the file path does not exist on disk, creates a new empty buffer with that filename (for creating new files)
C-g cancels the operation
Error messages (e.g., permission denied) are shown in the message line
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use NewBufferFromFile() for existing files. Compare against buf.Filename for each buffer to detect duplicates. For new files, create NewBuffer() and set its Filename field.

userStories.title: Buffer list display (C-x C-b)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a list of all open buffers so that I can choose which one to edit.
acceptanceCriteria: C-x C-b creates a special *Buffer List* buffer and switches to it
The buffer content lists all open buffers, one per line, showing: marker (> for current), modified flag (* or space), buffer name, and file path
If a *Buffer List* buffer already exists, it is updated rather than duplicated
The *Buffer List* buffer itself is not marked as modified
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create a formatted text buffer. The > marker should indicate which buffer was active before opening the buffer list. Regenerate content each time C-x C-b is pressed.

userStories.title: Close buffer (C-x k)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to close a buffer I no longer need so that my buffer list stays manageable.
acceptanceCriteria: C-x k activates minibuffer mode with prompt 'Kill buffer: (default CURRENT_NAME) '
Pressing Enter without input closes the current buffer; typing a name closes the specified buffer
If the target buffer has unsaved changes, shows 'Buffer modified; kill anyway? (y/n)' and waits for y/n
On 'y', the buffer is removed from the buffer list; on 'n', the operation is cancelled
If the last buffer is closed, a new empty *scratch* buffer replaces it
After closing, the active buffer switches to an adjacent buffer in the list
C-g cancels the operation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The y/n confirmation is a sub-mode within the minibuffer flow. After the minibuffer callback identifies the target buffer, check its Modified flag before removing.

userStories.title: Window struct and multi-window rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Window abstraction so that the screen can be divided into multiple independently-scrolling panes.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a Window struct with fields: buffer pointer (*Buffer), scroll offset (int), start row (int), and height (int, including status line)
main.go holds a windows list ([]*Window) and an active window index
Initially there is one window occupying the full screen (minus the message line)
The rendering pipeline loops over all windows, drawing each window's buffer content in its row range
Each window has its own status line at its bottom row showing the buffer name, modified flag, and cursor position
The active window's status line uses reverse video; inactive windows use a dimmer style (e.g., dashes instead of equals)
ScrollOffset is read from the Window struct, not from Buffer.ScrollOffset
The message line remains at the bottom row of the screen, shared across all windows
Cursor is shown only in the active window
Terminal resize recalculates all window row ranges
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a significant refactor of the rendering pipeline. Move ScrollOffset from Buffer to Window. The AdjustScroll logic must use the window's height (minus 1 for status line). Each window's viewHeight = window.height - 1. drawBuffer/drawBufferWithSearch must accept a row offset and height parameter.

userStories.title: Split window (C-x 2)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to split the screen so that I can view two buffers or two positions in the same buffer simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: C-x 2 splits the current window into two vertically stacked windows
Both new windows display the same buffer as the original window
Each window gets approximately half the original window's height
Each window has an independent scroll offset (initially both match the original)
Multiple splits are possible (3+ windows), each evenly dividing available height
The active window remains the top half after splitting
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Insert a new Window after the active window in the windows list, then recalculate all window heights by evenly distributing available screen rows.

userStories.title: Move focus between windows (C-x o) and close windows (C-x 0 / C-x 1)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to move focus between windows and close them to manage my layout.
acceptanceCriteria: C-x o moves focus to the next window in the list, cycling back to the first after the last
The cursor moves to the newly active window's buffer cursor position
The status line styling updates to reflect the new active window
C-x 0 closes the current window (buffer remains in buffer list). No-op if only one window exists
C-x 1 closes all windows except the current one (buffers remain in buffer list)
After closing windows, remaining windows redistribute screen height evenly
If a closed buffer (via C-x k) was displayed in a window, that window switches to another buffer from the buffer list
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: For C-x 0, remove the active window from the windows list and adjust activeWindowIdx. For C-x 1, replace the windows list with just the active window. Always recalculate window heights after changes.
